symptoms.symptom_name: Painless macroscopic haematuria with clot passage

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless visible haematuria is the hallmark presentation of urothelial malignancy.
Painless bleeding without infective symptoms argues against active infection as the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weeks of irritative voiding symptoms (dysuria, frequency, urgency) unresponsive to antibiotics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Irritative symptoms unresponsive to antibiotics can reflect carcinoma in situ and are commonly mislabelled as UTI.
Would explain irritative symptoms, but the failed antibiotic course lowers the likelihood.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss (~4 kg/2 months) with profound fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional symptoms raise concern for higher-stage or advanced malignancy.
Smoking is the dominant risk factor for urothelial carcinoma and markedly raises pre-test probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of colicky flank/loin pain and absence of fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symptomatic stones causing this degree of haematuria almost always produce colicky flank pain.
Absence of fever and loin pain argues against an infective/obstructive-infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Not taking any anticoagulant or antiplatelet medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excluding anticoagulant use removes a common benign contributor and keeps malignancy at the top of the list.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis — florid macroscopic haematuria without nitrites or leukocytes

investigation_details.expected_result: Florid blood; no nitrites, no leukocytes (does not support active infection)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot be performed by the AI Doctor; requires in-person urinalysis to help distinguish infective from non-infective haematuria.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent nitrites/leukocytes argue against active bacterial infection despite visible blood.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender abdomen with mild suprapubic fullness and no palpable mass

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild suprapubic fullness raises the possibility of early clot/retention; requires in-person examination and bladder scan to assess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Papillary mucosal mass on flexible cystoscopy

investigation_details.expected_result: Papillary mucosal mass consistent with urothelial carcinoma
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The definitive diagnostic step; unavailable via telehealth — the reason urgent fast-track urology referral is mandatory.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of a bladder tumour with biopsy is the confirmatory finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild tachycardia (HR 95) with maintained blood pressure (115/75)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia with a maintained BP suggests the patient is currently compensating; clinician-measured vitals are sealed and not available to the AI Doctor, who must infer risk from reported lightheadedness.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Painless visible haematuria in a smoker with constitutional symptoms
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Painless macroscopic haematuria + heavy smoking + unintentional weight loss is a high-probability urothelial-cancer cluster mandating urgent fast-track investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent flexible cystoscopy (via fast-track urology/haematuria clinic)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive assessment of the bladder mucosa for a tumour; the core reason an in-person urgent referral is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Upper-tract imaging (CT urogram or renal/bladder ultrasound)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Painless visible haematuria requires imaging of the upper tracts to exclude renal/ureteric urothelial or renal cell tumours.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, UEC and coagulation profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess haemoglobin/blood loss, renal function and clotting; arranged by the clinician, not obtainable via telehealth.
Supports the malignancy workup and helps exclude infection; requires a formal laboratory sample.
Treating painless visible haematuria as another UTI without cystoscopy is the classic error that delays cancer diagnosis.
